images.caption: Figure 1:Co-Directormulti-agent pipeline:(Top):The Orchestrator Agent (Φo​r​c​h\Phi_{orch}) utilizes MAB to navigate a factored creative action spaceθ=(θc​s,θn​m,θa​a)\theta=(\theta_{cs},\theta_{nm},\theta_{aa}).(Bottom):The Pre-Production Agent (Φp​r​e\Phi_{pre}) synthesizes a creative brief (ℬ\mathcal{B}), storyline (ℒ\mathcal{L}), and visual assets (𝒱\mathcal{V}) to establish a consistent storyboard (𝒮\mathcal{S}), which the Production Agent (Φp​r​o​d\Phi_{prod}) translates into synchronized keyframes (𝒦\mathcal{K}), video clips (𝒞\mathcal{C}), and audio (𝒜\mathcal{A}). An MLLM Judge evaluates the final video (FF) to generate a factored reward signalR=(rc​s,rn​m,ra​a)R=(r_{cs},r_{nm},r_{aa}), which is propagated back to the MAB to iteratively refine the creative configuration overTToptimization loops.
Figure 2:MAB Optimization Efficiency.Cumulative best performance over 10 iterations comparingCo-Director’s MAB optimization against an undirected Random Search.

Figure 3:Qualitative results onGenAd-Bench.Co-Directortranslates complex product prompts into cohesive visual narratives. The framework maintains character and product identity across dynamic poses and varied angles (top) and preserves precise product details during dramatic environmental and lighting transitions (bottom).

Figure 4:Additional qualitative results onGenAd-Bench.Co-Directoreffectively generates diverse visual narratives across both stereotypical and unconventional product scenarios. It successfully evokes expected atmospheres for stereotypical prompts, such as the rustic warmth of FredBaz-Syrup or the gentle nursery lighting of Xyzzy-Bear. The pipeline shows robustness to unconventional contexts, maintaining consistency in character/product identity and complex lighting (e.g., neon urban streets in EveFoo-Helmet) while executing precise object manipulation in unexpected environments like a library (LoremBaz-Pillow) or a high-tech workspace (Wandom-Glue).

header: Appendix CEvaluation on General Video Storytelling

images.caption: Figure 5:Qualitative results on ViStoryBench.Five consecutive frames are displayed for each generated story (01, 08, 27, 29, 32, 52, 55, 60, 64, 79). Across these diverse examples,Co-Directormaintains robust temporal consistency, successfully preserving both character identity and background spatial coherence throughout the sequences.

Figure 6:Qualitative results on ViStoryBench (Story 28).The generated sequence demonstrates strong prompt alignment and robust character consistency across frames, though minor anatomical artifacts (e.g., left-right hand confusion) occasionally occur.

Figure 7:GenAd-Benchreference visuals.The reference set comprises brand logos and product images, with each brand featuring four product pairs specifically curated to represent both stereotypical and unconventional target demographics.

Figure 8:Dataset Statistics forGenAd-Bench.The benchmark enforces rigorous categorical, demographic, and geographic diversity to prevent generative collapse. (a) The macro-category distribution spans a wide array of physical domains. (b) Target age demographics cover four distinct life stages. (c) Scenarios are situated across 183 unique global locations, grouped here into six continental macro-regions. (d) Target gender distributions are perfectly balanced at the product level to neutralize latent generation biases.

header: Appendix FExtended Discussion on MAB Optimization Efficiency

images.caption: Figure 9:Visual Guide to Aesthetic Archetypes (θa​a\theta_{aa}):This comparison shows how the same product is "re-shot" by the system to match different moods.(a) Clarity/Energy ("The Pop Look"):Everything is bright and easy to see. It uses "high-key" lighting with no dark shadows to make the product look clean, vibrant, and exciting.(b) Cinematic Premium ("The Movie Look"):This uses "Chiaroscuro" (dramatic light and deep shadows). It makes the product look expensive and legendary, like a hero in a high-end film.(c) Minimalist Focus ("The Gallery Look"):The background is deleted or made clean. The camera zooms in to show off tiny textures (like crystals or kibble pellets) in a calm, "museum-like" setting.(d) Kinetic Grit ("The Raw Look"):This feels like a real-world documentary. The lighting is dark and "moody," and the camera feels like it’s being held by a person (handheld) or a drone, creating a sense of raw intensity. Examples:Grault-Heel by Grault Design(ID: 34, Type: Stereotypical) andCon-Kibble by Consectetur Co.(ID: 96, Type: Unconventional).
